MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Aug-16-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Beth A. Huber of Huber Law Firm (415-456-4411) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Cristina L. Piechocki of Littler Mendelson PC (925-932-2468) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS CUSHMAN & WAKEFIELD, INC. AND CUSHMAN & WAKEFIELD OF CALIFORNIA, INC'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is DENIED. The complaint - which the Court is required to liberally construe - pleads that moving defendants acted as plaintiff's employers. (CCP 452; Cmplt. para. 4.) Factual issues not susceptible to judgment on the pleadings exist as to enterprise liability and/or joint employment. The cases defendants cite are inapposite. (See Laird v. Capital Cities/ABC, Inc. (1998) 68 Cal.App.4th 727 (summary judgment, not judgment on pleadings); Vernon v. State (2004) 116 Cal.App.4th 114 (plaintiff admits city was direct employer).) Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR #11332, Bus. Tel: 415-551-3745, email: lmartinez@sftc.org. (D302)
